scenario: Hard lockup due to spinlock deadlock with timer discrepancy
symptoms: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 22
Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP

workflow.thought: Check system information to understand environment
workflow.observation: BIOS version and date for hardware context

workflow.thought: Examine tasks in uninterruptible state to identify I/O bottlenecks
workflow.observation: Tasks stuck in I/O wait on CPU 22

workflow.thought: Analyze backtraces of uninterruptible tasks for common patterns
workflow.action: foreach UN bt | awk '/#[1-4] /{print $3,$5}' | paste - - - - | column -t
workflow.observation: Tasks waiting on sync_page and related functions

workflow.thought: Check device mapper logical volumes for storage issues
workflow.observation: Logical volumes and their open counts

workflow.thought: Examine SCSI commands for timeout issues
workflow.observation: Active SCSI commands with negative deadlines

workflow.thought: Inspect request queue timeout timer status
Check timer base values for CPU 72 to understand timer lag
workflow.observation: timer_jiffies value compared to current jiffies

workflow.thought: Get current jiffies counter value
Verify per-CPU timer base address for CPU 72
workflow.observation: Address of tvec_base structure for CPU 72

workflow.thought: Examine backtrace of CPU 72 to understand its state
workflow.observation: CPU 72 in idle state with mwait_idle_with_hints

workflow.thought: Inspect spinlock state that's causing the deadlock
workflow.observation: Spinlock raw_lock value showing waiter/serving tickets

workflow.thought: Disassemble hrtimer_cancel to understand code flow
Disassemble hrtimer_try_to_cancel entry point
Read memory to find hrtimer address from stack
Calculate spinlock address from hrtimer structure
Disassemble around hrtimer_try_to_cancel with line references
Examine hrtimer_force_reprogram for timer programming calls
Check tick_program_event for clock event programming
Examine clockevents_program_event for timer programming
workflow.observation: Function calls for clock event programming

workflow.thought: Disassemble mwait_idle_with_hints with line references
Get task stack address for thread_info inspection
Check thread_info flags for rescheduling status
workflow.observation: Thread flags value (8 indicates no reschedule needed)

workflow.thought: Examine task preemption count
Get current jiffies value again for timer comparison
Analyze all CPU timer bases for lag behind current jiffies
workflow.observation: Generate commands to check all timer bases

workflow.thought: Count exception RIP patterns across all CPUs
workflow.action: bt -a | grep "exception RIP:" | sort | uniq -c | sort -k1nr

workflow.thought: Check watchdog thread affinity masks for CPUs 64-79
workflow.action: foreach 'watchdog/*' task -R cpus_allowed.bits | paste - - - | awk '$8~/watchdog\//' | awk '{c="";for(i=1;i<12;i++)c=c $i" ";print c}'
workflow.observation: Watchdog thread affinity masks (empty for CPUs 64-79)

workflow.thought: Evaluate bitmask for CPU 63 to verify affinity

root_cause_analysis: The exact root cause is unknown at this time.
The hard lockup occurred on the CPU on which the task is looping in the spinlock in kernel mode without giving other tasks a chance to run for more than 60 seconds.
At that time another task had taken that spinlock and was running CPU idle routine.
At that time, almost all CPUs' tvec_base values were way behind the current jiffies value with the only exception of the one CPU on which the hard lockup occurred.
